steps.title: Find the gesture (pencil)
steps.description: Make sure you're in pencil mode. Lightly sketch a long shallow arc for the bottom of the hull — like a slice of melon. This single curve sets the boat's lean and posture.

steps.title: Block the hull (pencil)
steps.description: Still in pencil, close the top of the hull with a gentler curve, meeting your first arc at each end in a soft point. The bow (front) can rise a touch higher than the stern.

steps.title: Mark the mast (pencil)
steps.description: Sketch a tall vertical line rising from the middle of the hull. It should be roughly twice the length of the hull — masts are taller than you think.

steps.title: Block the sail (pencil)
steps.description: Sketch a tall triangle behind the mast — its long edge running along the mast, its top at the masthead, and its bottom corner extending out behind the boat. A gentle outward curve on the trailing edge suggests the sail catching air.

steps.title: Switch to ink and trace the hull and mast
steps.description: Switch to ink. Lay down the hull outline in one confident pass, then the mast in a single long stroke. Drawing from the shoulder helps the mast come out straight.

steps.title: Ink the sail (ink)
steps.description: Still in ink, trace the two free edges of the sail in confident strokes. Where the sail meets the mast, let the lines land cleanly at the same point.

steps.title: Suggest the rigging (ink)
steps.description: Add one or two short lines from the masthead down to the hull for rigging. Keep it sparse — a few suggestive marks read as rope without cluttering the boat.

steps.title: Clean up
steps.description: Switch to the eraser and remove any pencil guides that distract. The boat should feel like it's resting on the water, not floating in the sky.
